comments.body: The cop tried to mansplain it to the medics that it was a simple drugs matter, went wandering around the house with no protection. Lawyer : novichok was in the kitchen, the bedroom,….are you not lucky not to have been infected (by a toxin)? Yes we were very lucky. Lawyer did you follow the Jessops rules written for such a situation? No. Were you lacking in respect to the medics? Not that I recall.

comments.body:
Well they've got to pay for the doctors massive pay rise haven't they.

Doctors were already the highest paid public sector workers in the uk and had been on strike putting lives at risk.

The gods needs come before all else.

And these people love playing god.

And now people will be deprived of drug treatment that could dramatically increase their quality of life.

As. If the majority of doctors could care less as they've got their pay rise. You can't beat that fix that playing god gives you.

Shame on them.

I was ready to be outraged but slowing decline of Alzheimer's by 5-7 months really doesn't seem that great. 


Even if I was diagnosed myself, and had to get it private, I probably would only bother with it if I was a billionaire, just because I can. If I were well off enough to just about afford it I think I'd still prefer to just go on a holiday with my family or something and cherish the time I had left of cognisance.

It's a long time if you've got it.

I've worked with dementia sufferers and it's not good. It could sometimes take up to an hour cueing a memory out of someone in the final stages. And then it was gone again. Briefly they could recall. Then it was lost again. 

Six  Months is a lifetime.

This could also be a minimum

The NHS has absolutely no problem wasting millions on useless drugs like anti depressants. They dish out these things like smarties when there's no evidence they work. Even NICE state this.

But they still dish them out.

You can't put a price on life and sanity

comments.body: I see how this could be headline grabbing but pretty much, how ever deep you read the article or read up about the drug, you're not going to know about the cost and the real life benefits of the drug. I have heard that drug companies charge an extortionate amount for new drug's. Unfortunately, don't know.
